title: cyperus subrigidulus
intro: Cyperus subrigidulus, also known as the sedge, is a type of plant belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae) and is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cyperus subrigidulus include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and a wound healer. Cyperus subrigidulus can be prepared into herbal remedies in the form of infusions, decoctions, or ointments, allowing for a range of applications in traditional medicine.
